---
title: "Title of Project (TBD)\\vspace{-2cm}"
bibliography: references.bib
nocite: "@*"
csl: ieee.csl
params:
  RL: 0
  device: "gpu"
  cache_mode: "prefer_cache"    # prefer_cache | recompute_missing_only | force_recompute
  cache_version: "v1"
  artifact_tag: "default"
  participant_set: null         # null = RL preset default; "small_set" | "full_set"
  model_set: "smoke"            # smoke | core | all
                                # smoke = ar1 + arma_aic
                                # core/all = ar1, ar2, wear_observation, wear_latent,
                                #            ar2_wear_observation, ar2_wear_latent, arma_aic
  model_family: "ar1"
                                # model_family options:
                                #   "ar1"                  = AR(1) + empirical hour/day baseline
                                #   "ar2"                  = AR(2) + empirical hour/day baseline
                                #   "wear_observation"     = AR(1) + observation-level wear model
                                #   "wear_latent"          = AR(1) + latent wear-state model
                                #   "ar2_wear_observation" = AR(2) + observation-level wear model
                                #   "ar2_wear_latent"      = AR(2) + latent wear-state model
                                #   "arma_aic"             = ARMA(p, q) chosen by AIC for each participant
  pooling_mode: "all"  # single mode, comma-separated string, YAML list, or "all"
                                # pooling_mode options:
                                #   "unit_specific"
                                #   "global_shared_k"
                                #   "partial_pooled_k"
                                #   "all" = all three pooling modes above
                                # example YAML list:
                                # pooling_mode:
                                #   - "unit_specific"
                                #   - "global_shared_k"
                                #   - "partial_pooled_k"
  covariate_mode: "none"
  mask_regime: "random_hours"
  missing_fraction: 0.20
  mask_seed: 531
  n_mask_replicates: 1
  fit_scope: "both"
  section_scope: "all"
  global_seed: 531
  panel_chunk_size: null       # participant batch size for panel fits; null = RL preset default
  stage1_starts: null
  stage2_starts: null
  stage1_keep_top: null
  stage2_keep_top: null
  stage1_particles: null
  stage2_particles: null
  stage1_if2_iterations: null
  stage2_if2_iterations: null
  stage1_cooling_fraction: null
  stage2_cooling_fraction: null
  stage1_rw_sd_scale: null
  stage2_rw_sd_scale: null
  cooling_fraction: null       # shared fallback for both stages when stage-specific cooling is null
  rw_sd_scale: null            # shared fallback for both stages when stage-specific RW scale is null
  stage1_eval_particles: null
  stage2_eval_particles: null
  stage1_eval_reps: null
  stage2_eval_reps: null
  stage1_fresh_random_starts: null
  stage2_fresh_random_starts: null
  stage1_jittered_restarts: null
  stage2_jittered_restarts: null
  # Global search window overrides. Leave null to use the built-in defaults.
  # Only parameters active in the selected model / pooling mode matter.
  phi_window_low: null
  phi_window_high: null
  phi1_window_low: null
  phi1_window_high: null
  phi2_window_low: null
  phi2_window_high: null
  phi24_window_low: null
  phi24_window_high: null
  sigma_window_low: null
  sigma_window_high: null
  k_fitbit_window_low: null
  k_fitbit_window_high: null
  log_k_fitbit_window_low: null
  log_k_fitbit_window_high: null
  mu_log_k_fitbit_window_low: null
  mu_log_k_fitbit_window_high: null
  log_tau_log_k_fitbit_window_low: null
  log_tau_log_k_fitbit_window_high: null
  eta_wear_lag1_window_low: null
  eta_wear_lag1_window_high: null
  wear_phi_window_low: null
  wear_phi_window_high: null
  wear_sigma_window_low: null
  wear_sigma_window_high: null
execute:
  echo: false
format:
  pdf:
    include-in-header:
      text: |
        \RedeclareSectionCommand[beforeskip=0.5em, runin=false, afterskip=0em]{section}
        \usepackage{fullpage}
        \usepackage{wrapfig}
number-sections: true
---
## Abstract {.unnumbered}

[interface here:
selects RL = 0, 1, or 2
RL = 0 means]

```{python}
#| tags: [parameters]

RL = 0
device = "gpu"
cache_mode = "prefer_cache"
cache_version = "v1"
artifact_tag = "default"
participant_set = None
model_set = "smoke"
model_family = "ar1"
pooling_mode = "all"
covariate_mode = "none"
mask_regime = "random_hours"
missing_fraction = 0.20
mask_seed = 531
n_mask_replicates = 1
fit_scope = "both"
section_scope = "all"
global_seed = 531
panel_chunk_size = None
stage1_starts = None
stage2_starts = None
stage1_keep_top = None
stage2_keep_top = None
stage1_particles = None
stage2_particles = None
stage1_if2_iterations = None
stage2_if2_iterations = None
stage1_cooling_fraction = None
stage2_cooling_fraction = None
stage1_rw_sd_scale = None
stage2_rw_sd_scale = None
cooling_fraction = None
rw_sd_scale = None
stage1_eval_particles = None
stage2_eval_particles = None
stage1_eval_reps = None
stage2_eval_reps = None
stage1_fresh_random_starts = None
stage2_fresh_random_starts = None
stage1_jittered_restarts = None
stage2_jittered_restarts = None
phi_window_low = None
phi_window_high = None
phi1_window_low = None
phi1_window_high = None
phi2_window_low = None
phi2_window_high = None
phi24_window_low = None
phi24_window_high = None
sigma_window_low = None
sigma_window_high = None
k_fitbit_window_low = None
k_fitbit_window_high = None
log_k_fitbit_window_low = None
log_k_fitbit_window_high = None
mu_log_k_fitbit_window_low = None
mu_log_k_fitbit_window_high = None
log_tau_log_k_fitbit_window_low = None
log_tau_log_k_fitbit_window_high = None
eta_wear_lag1_window_low = None
eta_wear_lag1_window_high = None
wear_phi_window_low = None
wear_phi_window_high = None
wear_sigma_window_low = None
wear_sigma_window_high = None
```

```{python}
#| echo: false
#| warning: false
#| message: false
#| label: compute-interface

# COMPUTE INTERFACE / PARAMS
# RL DEFAULTS AND RESOLVED CONFIG
# DEVICE SETUP
# EXPERIMENT REGISTRY

import hashlib
import json
import os
import sys
from pathlib import Path

import pandas as pd
import yaml


PROJECT_ROOT = Path.cwd().resolve()
APP_CODE_ROOT = PROJECT_ROOT.parent / "eda"
HELPERS_ROOT = PROJECT_ROOT / "helpers"

if str(APP_CODE_ROOT) not in sys.path:
    sys.path.insert(0, str(APP_CODE_ROOT))


def _raw_params() -> dict:
    parameter_keys = [
        "RL",
        "device",
        "cache_mode",
        "cache_version",
        "artifact_tag",
        "participant_set",
        "model_set",
        "model_family",
        "pooling_mode",
        "covariate_mode",
        "mask_regime",
        "missing_fraction",
        "mask_seed",
        "n_mask_replicates",
        "fit_scope",
        "section_scope",
        "global_seed",
        "panel_chunk_size",
        "stage1_starts",
        "stage2_starts",
        "stage1_keep_top",
        "stage2_keep_top",
        "stage1_particles",
        "stage2_particles",
        "stage1_if2_iterations",
        "stage2_if2_iterations",
        "stage1_cooling_fraction",
        "stage2_cooling_fraction",
        "stage1_rw_sd_scale",
        "stage2_rw_sd_scale",
        "cooling_fraction",
        "rw_sd_scale",
        "stage1_eval_particles",
        "stage2_eval_particles",
        "stage1_eval_reps",
        "stage2_eval_reps",
        "stage1_fresh_random_starts",
        "stage2_fresh_random_starts",
        "stage1_jittered_restarts",
        "stage2_jittered_restarts",
        "phi_window_low",
        "phi_window_high",
        "phi1_window_low",
        "phi1_window_high",
        "phi2_window_low",
        "phi2_window_high",
        "phi24_window_low",
        "phi24_window_high",
        "sigma_window_low",
        "sigma_window_high",
        "k_fitbit_window_low",
        "k_fitbit_window_high",
        "log_k_fitbit_window_low",
        "log_k_fitbit_window_high",
        "mu_log_k_fitbit_window_low",
        "mu_log_k_fitbit_window_high",
        "log_tau_log_k_fitbit_window_low",
        "log_tau_log_k_fitbit_window_high",
        "eta_wear_lag1_window_low",
        "eta_wear_lag1_window_high",
        "wear_phi_window_low",
        "wear_phi_window_high",
        "wear_sigma_window_low",
        "wear_sigma_window_high",
    ]
    injected = {key: globals()[key] for key in parameter_keys if key in globals()}
    if injected:
        return injected
    try:
        raw = dict(params)
        if raw:
            return raw
    except Exception:
        pass
    qmd_path = PROJECT_ROOT / "computedraft.qmd"
    if not qmd_path.exists():
        raw = {}
        return raw
    try:
        text = qmd_path.read_text(encoding="utf-8")
        if not text.startswith("---"):
            return {}
        end_idx = text.find("\n---", 4)
        if end_idx < 0:
            return {}
        frontmatter = yaml.safe_load(text[4:end_idx]) or {}
        params_block = frontmatter.get("params", {})
        return dict(params_block) if isinstance(params_block, dict) else {}
    except Exception:
        return {}


def get_rl_defaults(rl: int) -> dict[str, object]:
    rl_int = int(rl)
    shared = {
        "cache_mode": "prefer_cache",
        "cache_version": "v1",
        "artifact_tag": "default",
        "model_family": "ar1",
        "pooling_mode": "unit_specific",
        "covariate_mode": "none",
        "mask_regime": "random_hours",
        "missing_fraction": 0.20,
        "mask_seed": 531,
        "n_mask_replicates": 1,
        "fit_scope": "both",
        "section_scope": "all",
        "global_seed": 531,
        "analysis_start_utc_iso": "2024-07-01T00:00:00+00:00",
        "analysis_end_utc_iso": "2024-09-30T23:00:00+00:00",
        "assumed_local_timezone": "America/New_York",
    }
    presets = {
        0: {
            **shared,
            "RL": 0,
            "device": "cpu",
            "participant_set": "small_set",
            "model_set": "smoke",
            "panel_chunk_size": 1,
            "stage1_starts": 1,
            "stage2_starts": 1,
            "stage1_keep_top": 1,
            "stage2_keep_top": 1,
            "stage1_particles": 20,
            "stage2_particles": 20,
            "stage1_if2_iterations": 1,
            "stage2_if2_iterations": 1,
            "stage1_cooling_fraction": 0.50,
            "stage2_cooling_fraction": 0.50,
            "stage1_rw_sd_scale": 0.02,
            "stage2_rw_sd_scale": 0.02,
            "cooling_fraction": 0.50,
            "rw_sd_scale": 0.02,
            "stage1_eval_particles": 20,
            "stage2_eval_particles": 20,
            "stage1_eval_reps": 1,
            "stage2_eval_reps": 1,
            "stage1_fresh_random_starts": 0,
            "stage2_fresh_random_starts": 0,
            "stage1_jittered_restarts": 0,
            "stage2_jittered_restarts": 0,
        },
        1: {
            **shared,
            "RL": 1,
            "device": "gpu",
            "participant_set": "full_set",
            "model_set": "smoke",
            "pooling_mode": "all",
            "panel_chunk_size": 78,
            "stage1_starts": 10,
            "stage2_starts": 5,
            "stage1_keep_top": 2,
            "stage2_keep_top": 1,
            "stage1_particles": 100,
            "stage2_particles": 100,
            "stage1_if2_iterations": 5,
            "stage2_if2_iterations": 5,
            "stage1_cooling_fraction": 0.90,
            "stage2_cooling_fraction": 0.90,
            "stage1_rw_sd_scale": 0.02,
            "stage2_rw_sd_scale": 0.015,
            "cooling_fraction": 0.90,
            "rw_sd_scale": 0.02,
            "stage1_eval_particles": 100,
            "stage2_eval_particles": 100,
            "stage1_eval_reps": 5,
            "stage2_eval_reps": 5,
            "stage1_fresh_random_starts": 0,
            "stage2_fresh_random_starts": 0,
            "stage1_jittered_restarts": 0,
            "stage2_jittered_restarts": 1,
        },
        2: {
            **shared,
            "RL": 2,
            "device": "gpu",
            "participant_set": "full_set",
            "model_set": "core",
            "panel_chunk_size": 4,
            "stage1_starts": 10,
            "stage2_starts": 4,
            "stage1_keep_top": 3,
            "stage2_keep_top": 2,
            "stage1_particles": 100,
            "stage2_particles": 160,
            "stage1_if2_iterations": 4,
            "stage2_if2_iterations": 8,
            "stage1_cooling_fraction": 0.50,
            "stage2_cooling_fraction": 0.50,
            "stage1_rw_sd_scale": 0.02,
            "stage2_rw_sd_scale": 0.02,
            "cooling_fraction": 0.50,
            "rw_sd_scale": 0.02,
            "stage1_eval_particles": 200,
            "stage2_eval_particles": 320,
            "stage1_eval_reps": 2,
            "stage2_eval_reps": 3,
            "stage1_fresh_random_starts": 0,
            "stage2_fresh_random_starts": 1,
            "stage1_jittered_restarts": 0,
            "stage2_jittered_restarts": 1,
        },
    }
    if rl_int not in presets:
        raise ValueError(f"Unsupported RL preset: {rl}")
    return presets[rl_int]


def resolve_config(raw_params: dict[str, object]) -> dict[str, object]:
    defaults = get_rl_defaults(int(raw_params.get("RL", 0)))
    resolved = dict(defaults)
    for key, value in raw_params.items():
        if value is None:
            continue
        if isinstance(value, str) and value == "":
            continue
        resolved[key] = value
    resolved["RL"] = int(resolved["RL"])
    resolved["missing_fraction"] = float(resolved["missing_fraction"])
    resolved["mask_seed"] = int(resolved["mask_seed"])
    resolved["n_mask_replicates"] = int(resolved["n_mask_replicates"])
    resolved["global_seed"] = int(resolved["global_seed"])
    if resolved.get("panel_chunk_size") is not None:
        resolved["panel_chunk_size"] = int(resolved["panel_chunk_size"])
    for name in (
        "stage1_starts",
        "stage2_starts",
        "stage1_keep_top",
        "stage2_keep_top",
        "stage1_particles",
        "stage2_particles",
        "stage1_if2_iterations",
        "stage2_if2_iterations",
        "stage1_eval_particles",
        "stage2_eval_particles",
        "stage1_eval_reps",
        "stage2_eval_reps",
        "stage1_fresh_random_starts",
        "stage2_fresh_random_starts",
        "stage1_jittered_restarts",
        "stage2_jittered_restarts",
    ):
        if resolved.get(name) is not None:
            resolved[name] = int(resolved[name])
    for name in (
        "cooling_fraction",
        "rw_sd_scale",
        "stage1_cooling_fraction",
        "stage2_cooling_fraction",
        "stage1_rw_sd_scale",
        "stage2_rw_sd_scale",
        "phi_window_low",
        "phi_window_high",
        "phi1_window_low",
        "phi1_window_high",
        "phi2_window_low",
        "phi2_window_high",
        "phi24_window_low",
        "phi24_window_high",
        "sigma_window_low",
        "sigma_window_high",
        "k_fitbit_window_low",
        "k_fitbit_window_high",
        "log_k_fitbit_window_low",
        "log_k_fitbit_window_high",
        "mu_log_k_fitbit_window_low",
        "mu_log_k_fitbit_window_high",
        "log_tau_log_k_fitbit_window_low",
        "log_tau_log_k_fitbit_window_high",
        "eta_wear_lag1_window_low",
        "eta_wear_lag1_window_high",
        "wear_phi_window_low",
        "wear_phi_window_high",
        "wear_sigma_window_low",
        "wear_sigma_window_high",
    ):
        if resolved.get(name) is not None:
            resolved[name] = float(resolved[name])
    if resolved.get("stage1_cooling_fraction") is None:
        resolved["stage1_cooling_fraction"] = float(resolved["cooling_fraction"])
    if resolved.get("stage2_cooling_fraction") is None:
        resolved["stage2_cooling_fraction"] = float(resolved["cooling_fraction"])
    if resolved.get("stage1_rw_sd_scale") is None:
        resolved["stage1_rw_sd_scale"] = float(resolved["rw_sd_scale"])
    if resolved.get("stage2_rw_sd_scale") is None:
        resolved["stage2_rw_sd_scale"] = float(resolved["rw_sd_scale"])
    for name in (
        "stage1_fresh_random_starts",
        "stage2_fresh_random_starts",
        "stage1_jittered_restarts",
        "stage2_jittered_restarts",
    ):
        if resolved.get(name) is None:
            resolved[name] = 0
    return resolved


RAW_PARAMS = _raw_params()
RESOLVED_CONFIG = resolve_config(RAW_PARAMS)

MODEL_SET_REGISTRY = {
    "smoke": ["ar1", "arma_aic"],
    "core": [
        "ar1",
        "ar2",
        "wear_observation",
        "wear_latent",
        "ar2_wear_observation",
        "ar2_wear_latent",
        "arma_aic",
    ],
    "all": [
        "ar1",
        "ar2",
        "wear_observation",
        "wear_latent",
        "ar2_wear_observation",
        "ar2_wear_latent",
        "arma_aic",
    ],
}

POOLING_MODE_OPTIONS = [
    "unit_specific",
    "global_shared_k",
    "partial_pooled_k",
]

GLOBAL_START_BOX_DEFAULTS = {
    "phi": (-0.95, 0.95),
    "phi1": (-1.25, 1.25),
    "phi2": (-0.99, 0.99),
    "phi24": (-0.99, 0.99),
    "sigma": (0.05, 2.00),
    "k_fitbit": (0.01, 10.0),
    "log_k_fitbit": (-3, 2),
    "mu_log_k_fitbit": (-3, 2),
    "log_tau_log_k_fitbit": (-2, 1),
    "eta_wear_lag1": (-4.0, 4.0),
    "wear_phi": (-0.99, 0.99),
    "wear_sigma": (0.05, 2.0),
}

WINDOW_PARAM_FIELDS = {
    "phi": ("phi_window_low", "phi_window_high"),
    "phi1": ("phi1_window_low", "phi1_window_high"),
    "phi2": ("phi2_window_low", "phi2_window_high"),
    "phi24": ("phi24_window_low", "phi24_window_high"),
    "sigma": ("sigma_window_low", "sigma_window_high"),
    "k_fitbit": ("k_fitbit_window_low", "k_fitbit_window_high"),
    "log_k_fitbit": ("log_k_fitbit_window_low", "log_k_fitbit_window_high"),
    "mu_log_k_fitbit": ("mu_log_k_fitbit_window_low", "mu_log_k_fitbit_window_high"),
    "log_tau_log_k_fitbit": ("log_tau_log_k_fitbit_window_low", "log_tau_log_k_fitbit_window_high"),
    "eta_wear_lag1": ("eta_wear_lag1_window_low", "eta_wear_lag1_window_high"),
    "wear_phi": ("wear_phi_window_low", "wear_phi_window_high"),
    "wear_sigma": ("wear_sigma_window_low", "wear_sigma_window_high"),
}


def _parameter_window_overrides(config: dict[str, object]) -> dict[str, tuple[float, float]]:
    overrides: dict[str, tuple[float, float]] = {}
    for param_name, (low_key, high_key) in WINDOW_PARAM_FIELDS.items():
        low_value = config.get(low_key)
        high_value = config.get(high_key)
        if low_value is None and high_value is None:
            continue
        default_low, default_high = GLOBAL_START_BOX_DEFAULTS[param_name]
        resolved_low = float(default_low if low_value is None else low_value)
        resolved_high = float(default_high if high_value is None else high_value)
        if resolved_low >= resolved_high:
            raise ValueError(f"Search window for {param_name} must satisfy low < high.")
        overrides[param_name] = (resolved_low, resolved_high)
    return overrides


def _active_model_families(config: dict[str, object]) -> list[str]:
    model_set_name = str(config["model_set"])
    if model_set_name not in MODEL_SET_REGISTRY:
        raise ValueError(f"Unknown model_set: {model_set_name}")
    families = list(MODEL_SET_REGISTRY[model_set_name])
    requested_family = str(config["model_family"])
    if requested_family not in families:
        families.append(requested_family)
    return families


def _active_pooling_modes(config: dict[str, object]) -> list[str]:
    raw_value = config.get("pooling_mode", "unit_specific")
    if isinstance(raw_value, str):
        stripped = raw_value.strip()
        if stripped.lower() == "all":
            requested = list(POOLING_MODE_OPTIONS)
        elif "," in stripped:
            requested = [part.strip() for part in stripped.split(",") if part.strip()]
        else:
            requested = [stripped]
    elif isinstance(raw_value, (list, tuple, set)):
        requested = [str(item).strip() for item in raw_value if str(item).strip()]
    else:
        requested = [str(raw_value).strip()]

    active_modes: list[str] = []
    for mode in requested:
        if mode not in POOLING_MODE_OPTIONS:
            raise ValueError(f"Unknown pooling_mode: {mode}")
        if mode not in active_modes:
            active_modes.append(mode)
    if not active_modes:
        raise ValueError("At least one pooling mode must be selected.")
    return active_modes


RESOLVED_CONFIG["active_model_families"] = _active_model_families(RESOLVED_CONFIG)
RESOLVED_CONFIG["active_pooling_modes"] = _active_pooling_modes(RESOLVED_CONFIG)
PARAMETER_WINDOW_OVERRIDES = _parameter_window_overrides(RESOLVED_CONFIG)
RESOLVED_CONFIG["search_windows"] = {
    **GLOBAL_START_BOX_DEFAULTS,
    **PARAMETER_WINDOW_OVERRIDES,
}

CACHE_ROOT = PROJECT_ROOT / "cache" / "computedraft" / str(RESOLVED_CONFIG["cache_version"]) / str(RESOLVED_CONFIG["artifact_tag"])
CACHE_PATHS = {
    "root": CACHE_ROOT,
    "base": CACHE_ROOT / "base",
    "masks": CACHE_ROOT / "masks",
    "fits_unmasked": CACHE_ROOT / "fits" / "unmasked",
    "fits_masked": CACHE_ROOT / "fits" / "masked",
    "benchmarks": CACHE_ROOT / "benchmarks",
    "derived": CACHE_ROOT / "derived",
    "manifests": CACHE_ROOT / "manifests",
    "logs": CACHE_ROOT / "logs",
}
for path in CACHE_PATHS.values():
    path.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
HELPERS_ROOT.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)

requested_device = str(RESOLVED_CONFIG["device"]).strip().lower()
if requested_device not in {"cpu", "gpu"}:
    raise ValueError(f"device must be 'cpu' or 'gpu', got {RESOLVED_CONFIG['device']!r}")
# JAX wants concrete backend names here. In our WSL + NVIDIA setup, asking for
# GPU should force CUDA rather than letting a stray ROCm probe interfere.
os.environ["JAX_PLATFORMS"] = "cuda" if requested_device == "gpu" else "cpu"

SEARCH_STAGE_SETTINGS = [
    {
        "name": "Stage 1",
        "n_starts": int(RESOLVED_CONFIG["stage1_starts"]),
        "keep_top": int(RESOLVED_CONFIG["stage1_keep_top"]),
        "particles": int(RESOLVED_CONFIG["stage1_particles"]),
        "mif_iterations": int(RESOLVED_CONFIG["stage1_if2_iterations"]),
        "cooling_fraction": float(RESOLVED_CONFIG["stage1_cooling_fraction"]),
        "rw_sd_scale": float(RESOLVED_CONFIG["stage1_rw_sd_scale"]),
        "evaluation_particles": int(RESOLVED_CONFIG["stage1_eval_particles"]),
        "evaluation_pfilter_reps": int(RESOLVED_CONFIG["stage1_eval_reps"]),
        "n_fresh_random_starts": int(RESOLVED_CONFIG["stage1_fresh_random_starts"]),
        "n_jittered_restarts": int(RESOLVED_CONFIG["stage1_jittered_restarts"]),
    },
    {
        "name": "Stage 2",
        "n_starts": int(RESOLVED_CONFIG["stage2_starts"]),
        "keep_top": int(RESOLVED_CONFIG["stage2_keep_top"]),
        "particles": int(RESOLVED_CONFIG["stage2_particles"]),
        "mif_iterations": int(RESOLVED_CONFIG["stage2_if2_iterations"]),
        "cooling_fraction": float(RESOLVED_CONFIG["stage2_cooling_fraction"]),
        "rw_sd_scale": float(RESOLVED_CONFIG["stage2_rw_sd_scale"]),
        "evaluation_particles": int(RESOLVED_CONFIG["stage2_eval_particles"]),
        "evaluation_pfilter_reps": int(RESOLVED_CONFIG["stage2_eval_reps"]),
        "n_fresh_random_starts": int(RESOLVED_CONFIG["stage2_fresh_random_starts"]),
        "n_jittered_restarts": int(RESOLVED_CONFIG["stage2_jittered_restarts"]),
    },
]

RESOLVED_CONFIG["config_hash"] = hashlib.sha256(
    json.dumps(RESOLVED_CONFIG, sort_keys=True, default=str).encode("utf-8")
).hexdigest()[:16]
AGGREGATE_PROGRESS_LOG_PATH = CACHE_PATHS["logs"] / f"aggregate_progress_{RESOLVED_CONFIG['config_hash']}.jsonl"
AGGREGATE_PROGRESS_MANIFEST_PATH = CACHE_PATHS["logs"] / f"aggregate_progress_manifest_{RESOLVED_CONFIG['config_hash']}.csv"

resolved_config_table = pd.DataFrame(
    [{"setting": key, "value": json.dumps(value, default=str) if isinstance(value, (dict, list, tuple)) else value} for key, value in RESOLVED_CONFIG.items()]
)
resolved_config_table.to_json(CACHE_PATHS["logs"] / f"resolved_config_{RESOLVED_CONFIG['config_hash']}.json", orient="records", indent=2)
```

# Introduction

## Background Information and Project Motivation

<!-- 
Intern Health Study (IHS) 2024 -> data on ~1000 new doctors

three domains (mood, sleep, and activity) but we are only focusing on activity

three wearable devies (garmin, apple, fitbit) -> disconnect in devices

at 3 pm, individuals have a 50% change of receiving a message (decided by an agent) 

goal: maximize step count which is the reward (I think?)
-->

The Intern Health Study (IHS) 2024 contains data on approximately 1,000 new doctors. @internhealthstudy The study collects information across three domains: mood, sleep, and physical activity. In this project, we focus only on physical activity which is measured using step count.

At 3:00 p.m. each day, an agent decides whether a participant receives a message, with a 50% probability of sending a message and a 50% probability of not sending one. This randomized setup allows researchers to study whether these messages influence participants' behavior. In this context, the objective is to increase physical activity, so step count serves as the reward outcome of interest.

<!-- 
RoME is a robust contextual bandit method for health apps that decides when to send notifications and how to personalize interventions based on the user’s current situation. @RoME

Oralytics, a mHealth intervention system, uses an online RL algorithm to determine optimal times for delivering interventions to promote oral self-care behaviors. @Oralytics

Missing data may result in distortions that alter algorithm behavior. Robust strategies are needed to handle delayed and missing data because the algorithm needs to continue functioning despite these challenges and ensure reproducibility. @DigitalHealth
-->

Reinforcement learning and contextual bandit methods are becoming increasingly important in healthcare because they enable personalized interventions. In digital health, these methods can be used to decide when and how to deliver prompts that encourage healthier behaviors, such as increasing physical activity or improving medication adherence. Contextual bandits are especially useful because they incorporate patient characteristics when selecting an action. For example, RoME is a robust contextual bandit method for mobile health applications that determines when to send notifications and how to personalize interventions based on a user’s current situation. @RoME Similarly, Oralytics is a mobile health intervention system that uses an online reinforcement learning algorithm to determine optimal times to deliver interventions that promote oral self-care behaviors. @Oralytics

This project is motivated by the growing need to apply these methods in realistic settings where data is often incomplete. In our dataset, missingness occurs when users do not wear their devices which leads to gaps in the recorded activity data. Missing data can distort the information available to the algorithm and may ultimately affect its behavior and performance. As a result, robust strategies for handling delayed and missing data are essential so that the algorithm can continue to function reliably and produce reproducible results. @DigitalHealth

## Past Project Context

<!-- 
1. [Heart Rate Variability](https://ionides.github.io/531w25/final_project/project10/blinded.pdf)

LG-POMP model; Apple measurements only
Peer review comments - more diagnostic plots, like looking at likelihood anomalies
No description of time zone handling
-->

Most past projects centered on infectious disease or financial topics. There was one project that also looked at measurements from wearable devices @HeartRate. That project focused on the instantaneous effect of environmental noise on heart rate variability (HRV), while ours is primarily about step count in the next 24 hours following a motivating message.

Both projects face the same issue of missing data. The HRV project pooled all participants together into a population-level daily summary, taking the median measurement of each day, while we initally designed POMP models for individual participants and then **[describe our pooling method]**. We investigate the effects of missingness on model performance by masking a percentage of the data, fitting a smoother, and calculating the RMSE. [**Haven't done yet** We also include other measurements from the wearable device as covariates in our model, which the HRV project did not do.] After reading past peer reviews, we made sure to add more diagnostic plots, including those describing likelihood anomalies and effective sample size.

## Research Question

When a participant does not wear the watch, no step count data are collected during that period. On days when the watch is worn for only a short time, or not at all, a reward cannot be constructed, so that day provides no training information for the model. The goal of this project is to build personalized POMP models for each doctor and use smoothing to estimate the missing rewards.

# Data Overview

## Data Description and Preprocessing

Participants wear devices from Garmin, Fitbit, and Apple, and some individuals may use more than one device. Since these devices differ in how they record and store data, the dataset contains some inconsistencies across devices. For example, Fitbit records data in local time, whereas Apple and Garmin use a global time zone. Additionally, the devices may also differ because of measurement noise.

## Multiple Device Analysis

For this project, we focused only on users who had Fitbit data for several reasons. Since not many users had Garmin devices, it would be difficult to train the model with such limited data. Of the approximately 1,000 doctors in the data, only 88 used Garmin. Apple HealthKit acts as a central repository for health and fitness data which combines information from both phones and watches. In other words, different apps are allowed to sync and aggregate data. For example, HealthKit can integrate with other wearables such as Garmin, so the step data may not even originate from an Apple Watch. It is too difficult to disentangle the biases associated with this device. A significant portion of people use multiple devices as can be seen in the table below.



<!--
| Apple | Fitbit | Garmin | Apple + Fitbit | Fitbit + Garmin | Apple + Garmin | All Three | Total
|---|---|---|
| 557 | 82 | C |
:  {#tbl-letters}
-->

(PRUNE FitbitHourly.csv to only July 1 to September 30, readjust back to local time)

The FitbitHourly.csv dataset contains wearable data from July 1, 2024 to September 30, 2024 for 299 participants. We analyze the 78 participants who have Fitbit steps data for at least half of their hourly rows. 

Notes:
* sample statistics like mean steps, std, mean steps per hourly non-missing bucket, missingness, maybe box plot for missingness (min, max, quartiles).

```{python}
# | echo: false
# | label: eda-libraries
import numpy as np
import pandas as pd
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import seaborn as sns
import re
```

```{python}
# | echo: false
# | label: eda-read-data
# read in dataset
fitbit_hourly = pd.read_csv("FitbitHourly.csv")
# fitbit_hourly.head()
```

```{python}
# | echo: false
# count how many participants are in the dataset
# num_participants = fitbit_hourly['PARTICIPANTIDENTIFIER'].nunique()
# print(f"There are {num_participants} participants in the dataset.")
```

```{python}
# | echo: false
# | label: fitbit-missing
# for each participant, get the mean value of the fitbitStepsMissing column (0 = step count recorded, 1 = missing) for a percent of how many hourly windows we have steps missing for.
fitbit_steps_missing_percent = (fitbit_hourly.groupby('PARTICIPANTIDENTIFIER')['fitbitStepsMissing'].mean() * 100).sort_values(ascending=True)

# # count how many have at least 50% of hours with a step count recorded
# num_participants_50_percent_fitbit = (fitbit_steps_missing_percent < 50).sum()
# print(f"{num_participants_50_percent_fitbit} participants have at least 50% of hours with a fitbit step count recorded.")
```

```{python}
# | echo: false
# | label: apple-missing
# repeat for apple
# apple_steps_missing_percent = (fitbit_hourly.groupby('PARTICIPANTIDENTIFIER')['appleStepsMissing'].mean() * 100).sort_values(ascending=True)

# print(apple_steps_missing_percent.head())

# # count how many have at least 50% of hours with a step count recorded
# num_participants_50_percent_apple = (apple_steps_missing_percent < 50).sum()
# print(f"{num_participants_50_percent_apple} participants have at least 50% of hours with a step count recorded with apple watch.")

```

```{python}
# | echo: false
# | label: missingness-histogram
# plot histogram of percent of hours with a step count missing for each participant for fitbit and apple
# add same y-axis limits for both plots for easier comparison

sns.histplot(fitbit_steps_missing_percent, bins=20, color='steelblue',
                edgecolor='black')
plt.xlabel('Percent of hours with a step count missing (Fitbit)')
plt.ylabel('Number of participants')
plt.title('Distribution of percent of hours with a step count missing for Fitbit')
plt.ylim(0, 205)
plt.show()

# sns.histplot(apple_steps_missing_percent, bins=20, color='steelblue',
#                 edgecolor='black')
# plt.xlabel('Percent of hours with a step count missing (Apple)')
# plt.ylabel('Number of participants')
# plt.title('Distribution of percent of hours with a step count missing for Apple')
# plt.ylim(0, 205)
# plt.show()

```

```{python}
# | echo: false
# | label: combined-device-missing
# If we combined step count metrics from fitbit and apple, how many participants would have at least 50% hours recorded?
# missing on "either device" means missing on BOTH devices that hour

# combined_missing_hour = fitbit_hourly[["fitbitStepsMissing", "appleStepsMissing"]].min(axis=1)  # 0 if either recorded, 1 if both missing

# # percent missing per participant
# combined_steps_missing_percent = (
#     combined_missing_hour
#     .groupby(fitbit_hourly["PARTICIPANTIDENTIFIER"])
#     .mean()
#     .mul(100)
#     .sort_values(ascending=True)
# )

# # 3) participants with at least 50% hours recorded => missing < 50%
# num_participants_50_percent_combined = (combined_steps_missing_percent < 50).sum()
# print(f"{num_participants_50_percent_combined} participants have at least 50% of hours with a step count recorded on either device.")
```

```{python}
# | echo: false

# how many participants have a recording for both devices (fitbit and apple) for at least 50% of hours?

# both_steps_missing_percent = (
#     both_missing_hour
#     .groupby(fitbit_hourly["PARTICIPANTIDENTIFIER"])
#     .mean()
#     .mul(100)
#     .sort_values(ascending=True)
# )

# print(f"{num_participants_50_percent_both} participants have at least 50% of hours with a step count recorded on both devices.")

# # Participant identifiers with at least 50% of hours recorded for both devices
# both_steps_missing_percent[both_steps_missing_percent < 50]
```


<!-- 
This is some UM-GPT code that is trying to plot the distribution of percent missingness for each combination of metric/device across participants. It's almost working, the issue is that the metric columns and the Missing columns have different names (e.g. appleHRAvg vs appleHRMissing (needs to be appleHRAvgMissing). If that gets corrected we could plot all of the metrics and use their missingness as extra justification for picking the steps as an outcome variable. Right now it only works for plotting steps. Also, some of the Missing columns have NaN instead of 0/1, which should probably be replace with 1 but should be double-checked.
-->

```{python}
# | echo: false
# df = fitbit_hourly

# ignore = {"moodScore", "messageSent", "messageStrategy"}

# # Missing-indicator columns (0 recorded, 1 missing; but may contain NaN)
# missing_cols = [c for c in df.columns if c.endswith("Missing") and c not in ignore]

# # IMPORTANT: if Missing columns have NaN (common when a device wasn't used),
# # decide how to treat them. Usually: NaN => missing (1).

#     return None, base

# # fraction missing per participant per metric/device

#     df2.groupby("PARTICIPANTIDENTIFIER")[missing_cols]
#        .mean()                       # mean of 0/1 = fraction missing
#        .reset_index()
#        .melt(id_vars="PARTICIPANTIDENTIFIER",
#              var_name="missing_col",
#              value_name="frac_missing")
# )

# long["pct_missing"] = 100 * long["frac_missing"]
# long[["device", "metric"]] = long["missing_col"].apply(lambda c: pd.Series(parse_missing_col(c)))

# # Choose which metrics to facet (these are the parsed metric names)
# metric_groups = ["Steps"]#, "HRAvg", "HRMin", "HRMax", "HRVAvg", "SleepMinutes"]
# plot_df = long[long["device"].notna() & long["metric"].isin(metric_groups)].copy()

# # Optional: nicer label for sleep
# plot_df["metric"] = plot_df["metric"].replace({"SleepMinutes": "SleepMinutes"})

# plot_df["device"] = pd.Categorical(plot_df["device"], ["apple", "fitbit", "garmin"], ordered=True)
# plot_df["metric"]  = pd.Categorical(plot_df["metric"], metric_groups, ordered=True)

# sns.set(style="whitegrid")

# g = sns.FacetGrid(
#     plot_df,
#     row="metric",
#     col="device",
#     margin_titles=True,
#     height=2.4,
#     aspect=1.4,
#     sharex=True,
#     sharey=True
# )
# g.map_dataframe(
#     sns.histplot,
#     x="pct_missing",
#     bins=20,
#     color="steelblue",
#     edgecolor="black"
# )

# g.set_axis_labels("Percent missing (per participant)", "Participants")
# g.set_titles(row_template="{row_name}", col_template="{col_name}")
# plt.tight_layout()
# plt.show()
```



### Model

Our aim is to predict the "24-hour-reward", the sum of the steps in the 24 hours after 3pm local time. Each of these hours is a bucket, where the Fitbit data may be observed, or it may be missing, which we model with a Partially Observed Markov Process (POMP) model. We try several configurations and benchmark them, but they all descend from the following design:

Latent process:
$$
x_{t+1} = \phi x_t + \sigma \epsilon_t, 
\qquad \epsilon_t \sim N(0, 1)
$$
$$
N_t | x_t \sim \text{Poisson}(\lambda, t),
\qquad \log \lambda_t = \alpha_{h(t)} + \delta_{w(t)} + x_t + \beta^\intercal z_t
$$

$$
Y_t | N_t \sim \text{NegBin}(N_t, k)
$$

Our aim is to estimate the step count 24 hours after 3pm local time each day:
$$
R_d = \sum \limits_{t \in H(d)} N_t
$$

The latent time model is a simple AR(1). The other components of the $\lambda_t$ are $\alpha_{h(t)}$, a personalized time of day factor, $\delta_{w(t)}$ a personalized day of week factor, and $\beta^\intercal z_t$, which are any covariates we decide to add. The $\alpha_{h(t)}$ and $\delta_{w(t)}$ are computed by simple averages of their measured step counts in the given hourly bucket. That is, $h(t)$ is the local hour of the day of time point $t$, and $w(t)$ is the day of the week of the time point $t$, and  $\alpha_{13}$ means the average step count in the 13th hourly bucket each day for this user, and $\delta_4$ means the average step count on the 4th day of the week (Thursday).

Because $\log \lambda_t$ is a linear model, we'll do variable selection for the features chosen in $z_t$ via a linear model. Specifically, we'll do it in a personalized way across all participants, using LASSO. Across a large set of candidate features, run a regression, use cross validation to pick the best penalty value. The variables which vanish under this penalty we don't pick, the variables which stay, we do pick. We run this across all participants being studied, so each user has a personalized feature set.

In the observation distributions, picking $k$ is not so clear, and perhaps the negative binomial distribution isn't the right choice. Its variance is: $\text{Var}(Y_t | N_t) = N_t + \frac{N^2_t}{k}$. When $k \rightarrow \infty$, then the variance $\rightarrow N_t$, and the distribution has the same mean and variance, which is to say, it's Poisson. So for $k$ large, we have Poisson distribution. But from running a few of these, $k$ seems to work best being not very large, like in the neighborhood of 40. Not sure what to make of it.

Our interpretation of $k$ is that it's some kind of "how trustworthy is a Fitbit measurement?" This is a bit problematic, because our latent is Fitbit steps. That said, we're thinking of this as a property _of_ the fitbit device, so it should be shared across participants. We need an experiment or setup that either estimates what this should be across everyone, -or- we should do a sort of partial pooling across everyone, so $k \sim N(\mu_F, \sigma^2)$ where the distribution is shared across everyone, or the mean is different but the variance is shared, or something. Regardless, we'd have to estimate either $k$ for everyone, or the paramaters for its distribution. Perhaps we could do this in some Bayesian way.

ALSO try ARMA

(EXPLAIN other model configurations)



### Benchmarks

Finding the best way to benchmark these models was a challenge. We employ a few measures of model performance: 1) Log-likelihood (uses latent, not prediction), 2) hourly masked RMSE (prediction vs observed),  3) masked RMSE on 24-hour rewards (prediction vs latent), 4) and masked RMSE on 24-hour (prediction vs observation).

Because the end-goal is prediction, not inference, we aim for the model that predicts unseen data the best, and employ a data masking regime. We mask 20% of the available data, fit the models, and observe error in predicting the known, but masked, data. (We also observe how different levels of masking affect the result).

For pedagogical purposes, we visualize individual and group level models with the simpler AR(1) configuration, but report benchmarks on each of these models at the end.

## Limitations of Benchmark

# Methodology and Results





## Individual

Here is what it looks like to run this model on an individual

(ADD Parameter start box for other models)

```{python}
#| warning: false
#| message: false
#| echo: false
#| label: set-controls
# (set XDG_RUNTIME_DIR=HW8 in bash to render quarto on greatlakes)

# Cache controls
enable_shelve_cache = True
clear_shelve_cache = False
cache_version_token = f"highmiss_cache_{RESOLVED_CONFIG['cache_version']}"
shelve_cache_directory = ".highmiss_cache"
shelve_cache_basename = f"highmiss_{RESOLVED_CONFIG['config_hash']}"

# Participant and model controls
participant_id = "MDH-3531-4796"
analysis_start_utc_iso = str(RESOLVED_CONFIG["analysis_start_utc_iso"])
analysis_end_utc_iso = str(RESOLVED_CONFIG["analysis_end_utc_iso"])
participant_data_lookback_hours = 48
assumed_local_timezone = str(RESOLVED_CONFIG["assumed_local_timezone"])
participant_covariate_keys = []  # Example: ["trail_steps_24h", "send_lag_0_3h"]
estimated_free_params = ["phi", "sigma", "k_fitbit"]
initial_phi = 0.75
initial_sigma = 0.30
initial_k_fitbit = 200.0

# Local MLE controls
local_mle_particles = int(RESOLVED_CONFIG["stage1_particles"])
local_mle_mif_iterations = int(RESOLVED_CONFIG["stage1_if2_iterations"])
local_mle_random_seed = int(RESOLVED_CONFIG["global_seed"])
local_mle_cooling_fraction = float(RESOLVED_CONFIG["stage1_cooling_fraction"])
local_mle_rw_sd_scale = float(RESOLVED_CONFIG["stage1_rw_sd_scale"])
local_mle_evaluation_particles = int(RESOLVED_CONFIG["stage1_eval_particles"])
local_mle_evaluation_pfilter_reps = int(RESOLVED_CONFIG["stage1_eval_reps"])

# Global MLE search controls
global_search_random_seed = int(RESOLVED_CONFIG["global_seed"]) + 26
global_search_include_current_theta_as_start = True
global_search_jitter_scale = 0.10
global_search_box_shrink_factor_per_stage = 1.00

# Global MLE stage 1 controls
global_stage1_name = "Stage 1"
global_stage1_n_starts = int(RESOLVED_CONFIG["stage1_starts"])
global_stage1_keep_top = int(RESOLVED_CONFIG["stage1_keep_top"])
global_stage1_particles = int(RESOLVED_CONFIG["stage1_particles"])
global_stage1_mif_iterations = int(RESOLVED_CONFIG["stage1_if2_iterations"])
global_stage1_cooling_fraction = float(RESOLVED_CONFIG["stage1_cooling_fraction"])
global_stage1_rw_sd_scale = float(RESOLVED_CONFIG["stage1_rw_sd_scale"])
global_stage1_evaluation_particles = int(RESOLVED_CONFIG["stage1_eval_particles"])
global_stage1_evaluation_pfilter_reps = int(RESOLVED_CONFIG["stage1_eval_reps"])
global_stage1_n_fresh_random_starts = int(RESOLVED_CONFIG["stage1_fresh_random_starts"])
global_stage1_n_jittered_restarts = int(RESOLVED_CONFIG["stage1_jittered_restarts"])

# Global MLE stage 2 controls
global_stage2_name = "Stage 2"
global_stage2_n_starts = int(RESOLVED_CONFIG["stage2_starts"])
global_stage2_keep_top = int(RESOLVED_CONFIG["stage2_keep_top"])
global_stage2_particles = int(RESOLVED_CONFIG["stage2_particles"])
global_stage2_mif_iterations = int(RESOLVED_CONFIG["stage2_if2_iterations"])
global_stage2_cooling_fraction = float(RESOLVED_CONFIG["stage2_cooling_fraction"])
global_stage2_rw_sd_scale = float(RESOLVED_CONFIG["stage2_rw_sd_scale"])
global_stage2_evaluation_particles = int(RESOLVED_CONFIG["stage2_eval_particles"])
global_stage2_evaluation_pfilter_reps = int(RESOLVED_CONFIG["stage2_eval_reps"])
global_stage2_n_fresh_random_starts = int(RESOLVED_CONFIG["stage2_fresh_random_starts"])
global_stage2_n_jittered_restarts = int(RESOLVED_CONFIG["stage2_jittered_restarts"])

# Global start-box controls
global_start_box_phi_low = float(RESOLVED_CONFIG["search_windows"]["phi"][0])
global_start_box_phi_high = float(RESOLVED_CONFIG["search_windows"]["phi"][1])
global_start_box_sigma_low = float(RESOLVED_CONFIG["search_windows"]["sigma"][0])
global_start_box_sigma_high = float(RESOLVED_CONFIG["search_windows"]["sigma"][1])
global_start_box_k_fitbit_low = float(RESOLVED_CONFIG["search_windows"]["k_fitbit"][0])
global_start_box_k_fitbit_high = float(RESOLVED_CONFIG["search_windows"]["k_fitbit"][1])

# Reconstructed reward plot controls
reward_plot_fit_source = "global"  # "local" or "global"
reward_plot_fig_width = 18.0
reward_plot_fig_height = 9
reward_plot_title_fontsize = 25
reward_plot_axis_label_fontsize = 22
reward_plot_tick_fontsize = 20
reward_plot_legend_fontsize = 20

# Residual-vs-missingness plot controls
residual_plot_fit_source = "global"  # "local" or "global"
residual_plot_fig_width = 5
residual_plot_fig_height = 4
```

```{python}
#| warning: false
#| message: false
#| echo: false
#| output: asis

# Everything needed to run the analysis is defined in this qmd. The only data
# file used below is FitbitHourly.csv.
import copy
from functools import lru_cache
import hashlib
import json
from pathlib import Path
import shelve
from dataclasses import dataclass
from time import perf_counter

import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import numpy as np
import pandas as pd
from IPython.display import display
import jax
import jax.numpy as jnp
from jax.scipy.special import gammaln
from pypomp import Pomp, ParTrans, RWSigma
try:
    from pypomp.random import fast_approx_rnbinom, fast_approx_rpoisson
except Exception:
    fast_approx_rnbinom = None
    fast_approx_rpoisson = None

FAST_QMD_SAMPLERS_AVAILABLE = bool(
    fast_approx_rpoisson is not None and fast_approx_rnbinom is not None
)

JAX_BACKEND = str(jax.default_backend())
if requested_device == "gpu":
    gpu_devices = [device for device in jax.devices() if device.platform == "gpu"]
    if not gpu_devices or JAX_BACKEND != "gpu":
        raise RuntimeError(
            "device='gpu' was requested in computedraft.qmd, but JAX did not initialize a GPU backend. "
            f"default_backend={JAX_BACKEND!r}, devices={[device.platform for device in jax.devices()]!r}"
        )
elif JAX_BACKEND != "cpu":
    raise RuntimeError(
        "device='cpu' was requested in computedraft.qmd, but JAX initialized a different backend. "
        f"default_backend={JAX_BACKEND!r}"
    )

# CHANGE PATH
CSV_PATH = Path("FitbitHourly.csv")
if not CSV_PATH.exists():
    raise FileNotFoundError("FitbitHourly.csv must be in the same folder as this qmd.")

CSV_CACHE_SIGNATURE = {
    "csv_size_bytes": int(CSV_PATH.stat().st_size),
    "csv_modified_ns": int(CSV_PATH.stat().st_mtime_ns),
}
SHELVE_CACHE_DIR = Path(shelve_cache_directory)
SHELVE_CACHE_PATH = SHELVE_CACHE_DIR / shelve_cache_basename
if enable_shelve_cache:
    SHELVE_CACHE_DIR.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    if clear_shelve_cache:
        with shelve.open(str(SHELVE_CACHE_PATH)) as cache_db:
            cache_db.clear()

CSV_COLUMNS = [
    "PARTICIPANTIDENTIFIER",
    "STUDY_USER_ID",
    "time_utc",
    "date_utc",
    "hour_of_day_utc",
    "fitbitSteps",
    "fitbitHRAvg",
    "fitbitSleepMinutes",
    "moodScore",
    "messageSent",
]

def normalize_timestamp_to_utc(value: str) -> pd.Timestamp:
    """Parse a timestamp string and ensure it is UTC-aware."""
    timestamp = pd.Timestamp(value)
    if timestamp.tzinfo is None:
        return timestamp.tz_localize("UTC")
    return timestamp.tz_convert("UTC")


def _cache_key_default(value):
    if isinstance(value, pd.Timestamp):
        return value.isoformat()
    if isinstance(value, Path):
        return str(value)
    if isinstance(value, (np.integer, np.floating)):
        return value.item()
    if isinstance(value, tuple):
        return list(value)
    raise TypeError(f"Object of type {type(value).__name__} is not JSON serializable.")


def build_cache_key(cache_name: str, **cache_inputs) -> str:
    payload = {
        "cache_name": cache_name,
        "cache_version_token": cache_version_token,
        "csv_signature": CSV_CACHE_SIGNATURE,
        "cache_inputs": cache_inputs,
    }
    encoded = json.dumps(payload, sort_keys=True, default=_cache_key_default).encode("utf-8")
    return f"{cache_name}:{hashlib.sha256(encoded).hexdigest()}"


def get_or_compute_shelve(cache_name: str, cache_inputs: dict, compute_fn, *, prepare_for_store=None):
    """Read a value from the shelve cache or compute and store it."""
    cache_key = build_cache_key(cache_name, **cache_inputs)
    if enable_shelve_cache:
        with shelve.open(str(SHELVE_CACHE_PATH)) as cache_db:
            if cache_key in cache_db:
                return cache_db[cache_key]
    value = compute_fn()
    stored_value = prepare_for_store(value) if prepare_for_store is not None else value
    if enable_shelve_cache:
        with shelve.open(str(SHELVE_CACHE_PATH)) as cache_db:
            cache_db[cache_key] = stored_value
    return stored_value


def display_table(
    df: pd.DataFrame,
    *,
    precision: int = 3,
    formatters: dict | None = None,
) -> None:
    """Render tables without row indexes in the compiled document."""
    styled = df.style.hide(axis="index")
    if formatters is not None:
        styled = styled.format(formatters, na_rep="")
    else:
        styled = styled.format(precision=precision, na_rep="")
    display(styled)


def format_table_value(value, precision: int = 3) -> str:
    """Format table values compactly for LaTeX output."""
    if pd.isna(value):
        return ""
    if isinstance(value, (int, np.integer)):
        return str(int(value))
    if isinstance(value, (float, np.floating)):
        numeric_value = float(value)
        if np.isfinite(numeric_value) and abs(numeric_value - round(numeric_value)) < 1e-9:
            return str(int(round(numeric_value)))
        return f"{numeric_value:.{precision}f}".rstrip("0").rstrip(".")
    return str(value)


def dataframe_to_latex_tabular(df: pd.DataFrame, *, precision: int = 3) -> str:
    """Convert a dataframe into a compact LaTeX tabular block."""
    formatted = df.copy()
    for column in formatted.columns:
        formatted[column] = formatted[column].map(lambda value: format_table_value(value, precision))
    return formatted.to_latex(index=False, escape=True)


def render_side_by_side_tables_latex(table_specs: list[dict]) -> str:
    """Render several small tables side by side using LaTeX minipages."""
    rendered_blocks = []
    for spec in table_specs:
        tabular_latex = dataframe_to_latex_tabular(spec["df"], precision=spec.get("precision", 3))
        rendered_blocks.append(
            "\n".join(
                [
                    rf"\begin{{minipage}}[t]{{{spec['width']}\linewidth}}",
                    r"\vspace{0pt}",
                    r"\centering",
                    rf"\captionof{{table}}{{{spec['caption']}}}",
                    r"\scriptsize",
                    tabular_latex,
                    r"\end{minipage}",
                ]
            )
        )
    return "\n\\hfill\n".join(rendered_blocks)


def make_key_value_table(
    mapping: dict[str, object],
    *,
    key_label: str = "Setting",
    value_label: str = "Value",
) -> pd.DataFrame:
    """Render wide configuration dictionaries as narrow two-column tables."""
    return pd.DataFrame(
        [{key_label: str(key), value_label: value} for key, value in mapping.items()]
    )


def select_table_columns(
    df: pd.DataFrame,
    columns: list[str],
    *,
    rename: dict[str, str] | None = None,
) -> pd.DataFrame:
    """Select only the columns that exist, optionally applying compact labels."""
    available = [column for column in columns if column in df.columns]
    if not available:
        return pd.DataFrame()
    selected = df.loc[:, available].copy()
    if rename is not None:
        selected = selected.rename(columns={key: value for key, value in rename.items() if key in selected.columns})
    return selected


def build_model_comparison_tables(df: pd.DataFrame) -> list[tuple[str, pd.DataFrame]]:
    """Split benchmark summaries into narrow tables that fit in the PDF."""
    if df.empty:
        return []
    tables: list[tuple[str, pd.DataFrame]] = []
    benchmark_column = "benchmark_label" if "benchmark_label" in df.columns else None
    benchmark_groups = (
        df.groupby(benchmark_column, dropna=False) if benchmark_column is not None else [("Benchmarks", df)]
    )
    rename = {
        "model_family": "model",
        "pooling_mode": "pooling",
        "benchmark_key": "benchmark",
        "benchmark_label": "benchmark",
        "hourly_masked_rmse": "hourly RMSE",
        "hourly_masked_mae": "hourly MAE",
        "hourly_masked_correlation": "hourly corr",
        "heldout_subtotal_rmse_sqrtm": "daily RMSE / sqrt(m)",
        "mean_signed_subtotal_error": "mean subtotal err",
        "mean_signed_normalized_subtotal_error": "mean norm err",
        "mean_panel_unit_loglik": "mean logLik",
        "n_windows_used": "windows",
        "mean_masked_hours": "mean masked hrs",
    }
    for benchmark_label, benchmark_frame in benchmark_groups:
        table_specs = [
            (
                f"{benchmark_label or 'Benchmarks'}",
                [
                    "model_family",
                    "pooling_mode",
                    "hourly_masked_rmse",
                    "hourly_masked_mae",
                    "hourly_masked_correlation",
                    "heldout_subtotal_rmse_sqrtm",
                ],
            ),
            (
                f"{benchmark_label or 'Benchmarks'} support",
                [
                    "model_family",
                    "pooling_mode",
                    "mean_panel_unit_loglik",
                    "n_windows_used",
                    "mean_masked_hours",
                ],
            ),
        ]
        for title, columns in table_specs:
            table = select_table_columns(benchmark_frame, columns, rename=rename)
            if table.empty:
                continue
            tables.append((title, table))
    return tables


def build_parameter_tables(df: pd.DataFrame) -> list[tuple[str, pd.DataFrame]]:
    """Split participant-level estimates into narrower thematic tables."""
    if df.empty:
        return []
    frame = df.copy()
    if "log_tau_log_k_fitbit" in frame.columns and "tau_log_k_fitbit" not in frame.columns:
        frame["tau_log_k_fitbit"] = np.exp(pd.to_numeric(frame["log_tau_log_k_fitbit"], errors="coerce"))

    rename = {
        "participantidentifier": "participant_id",
        "participant_id": "participant_id",
        "model_family": "model",
        "pooling_mode": "pooling",
        "k_fitbit_pooling_mode": "k_pool",
        "panel_unit_loglik": "loglik",
        "log_k_fitbit": "log_k",
        "mu_log_k_fitbit": "mu_log_k",
        "tau_log_k_fitbit": "tau_log_k",
        "log_tau_log_k_fitbit": "log_tau_k",
        "arma_p": "AR p",
        "arma_q": "MA q",
        "aic": "AIC",
    }
    frame = frame.rename(columns={key: value for key, value in rename.items() if key in frame.columns})

    blocks: list[tuple[str, pd.DataFrame]] = []
    for title, columns in [
        ("Participant fit summary", ["participant_id", "model", "pooling", "k_pool", "loglik"]),
        ("Dynamic parameters", ["participant_id", "model", "sigma", "phi", "phi1", "phi2", "phi24"]),
        ("Wear parameters", ["participant_id", "model", "wear_phi", "wear_sigma"]),
        ("k-related parameters", ["participant_id", "model", "k_fitbit", "log_k", "mu_log_k", "tau_log_k"]),
        ("ARMA parameters", ["participant_id", "model", "AR p", "MA q", "AIC", "loglik"]),
    ]:
        block = select_table_columns(frame, columns)
        informative_cols = [column for column in block.columns if column not in {"participant_id", "model"}]
        if not block.empty and informative_cols:
            blocks.append((title, block))
    return blocks


def build_task_manifest_table(df: pd.DataFrame) -> pd.DataFrame:
    """Reduce the task manifest to the fields that matter in the report."""
    if df.empty:
        return df.copy()
    frame = df.copy()
    if "task_id" in frame.columns:
        frame["task"] = (
            frame["task_id"]
            .astype(str)
            .str.replace("::mask_rep_", " / rep ", regex=False)
            .str.replace("::", " / ", regex=False)
            .str.replace("benchmark", "bench", regex=False)
            .str.replace("unmasked", "unmask", regex=False)
            .str.replace("masked", "mask", regex=False)
            .str.replace("unit_specific", "unit", regex=False)
            .str.replace("global_shared_k", "global_k", regex=False)
            .str.replace("partial_pooled_k", "partial_k", regex=False)
            .str.replace("core_masking", "core", regex=False)
        )
    if "mask_replicate" in frame.columns:
        frame["mask_replicate"] = frame["mask_replicate"].fillna("-")
    rename = {
        "benchmark_key": "benchmark",
        "runtime_seconds": "runtime (s)",
    }
    columns = ["task", "status", "runtime_seconds"]
    if "benchmark_key" in frame.columns and frame["benchmark_key"].notna().any():
        columns.insert(1, "benchmark_key")
    return select_table_columns(
        frame,
        columns,
        rename=rename,
    )


def build_timing_table(df: pd.DataFrame) -> pd.DataFrame:
    """Keep the timing summary compact for PDF output."""
    if df.empty:
        return df.copy()
    rename = {
        "model_family": "model",
        "masked_runtime_seconds": "masked runtime (s)",
        "unmasked_runtime_seconds": "unmasked runtime (s)",
        "total_runtime_seconds": "total runtime (s)",
        "artifact_size_mb": "size (MB)",
    }
    return select_table_columns(
        df,
        [
            "model_family",
            "unmasked_runtime_seconds",
            "masked_runtime_seconds",
            "total_runtime_seconds",
            "artifact_size_mb",
        ],
        rename=rename,
    )


def _comparable_numeric_frame(truth: pd.Series, estimate: pd.Series) -> pd.DataFrame:
    comparable = pd.DataFrame(
        {
            "truth": pd.to_numeric(truth, errors="coerce"),
            "estimate": pd.to_numeric(estimate, errors="coerce"),
        }
    ).dropna()
    if comparable.empty:
        return comparable
    finite_mask = np.isfinite(comparable["truth"]) & np.isfinite(comparable["estimate"])
    return comparable.loc[finite_mask].reset_index(drop=True)


def _safe_rmse(truth: pd.Series, estimate: pd.Series) -> float:
    comparable = _comparable_numeric_frame(truth, estimate)
    if comparable.empty:
        return float("nan")
    return float(np.sqrt(np.mean(np.square(comparable["estimate"] - comparable["truth"]))))


def _safe_corr(truth: pd.Series, estimate: pd.Series) -> float:
    comparable = _comparable_numeric_frame(truth, estimate)
    if len(comparable) < 2:
        return float("nan")
    return float(comparable["truth"].corr(comparable["estimate"]))


def summarize_hourly_metrics_by_participant(hourly_frame: pd.DataFrame) -> pd.DataFrame:
    if hourly_frame.empty or "participant_id" not in hourly_frame.columns:
        return pd.DataFrame(columns=["participant_id", "hourly_rmse", "hourly_correlation", "hourly_benchmark_hours"])
    benchmark = build_masked_hourly_benchmark_frame(
        hourly_frame,
        truth_column="fitbit_truth_for_eval",
        mask_flag_column="fitbit_masked_for_eval",
        positive_truth_only=True,
        estimate_columns=["latent_fitbit_scale_steps"],
    )
    if benchmark.empty:
        return pd.DataFrame(columns=["participant_id", "hourly_rmse", "hourly_correlation", "hourly_benchmark_hours"])
    rows: list[dict[str, object]] = []
    for participant_id, part in benchmark.groupby("participant_id", dropna=False):
        truth = pd.to_numeric(part["benchmark_truth_for_eval"], errors="coerce")
        estimate = pd.to_numeric(part["latent_fitbit_scale_steps"], errors="coerce")
        rows.append(
            {
                "participant_id": str(participant_id),
                "hourly_rmse": _safe_rmse(truth, estimate),
                "hourly_correlation": _safe_corr(truth, estimate),
                "hourly_benchmark_hours": int(_comparable_numeric_frame(truth, estimate).shape[0]),
            }
        )
    return pd.DataFrame(rows)


def summarize_reward_metrics_by_participant(reward_frame: pd.DataFrame) -> pd.DataFrame:
    if reward_frame.empty or "participant_id" not in reward_frame.columns:
        return pd.DataFrame(columns=["participant_id", "reward_rmse", "daily_correlation", "reward_windows_compared"])
    rows: list[dict[str, object]] = []
    for participant_id, part in reward_frame.groupby("participant_id", dropna=False):
        truth = pd.to_numeric(part["observed_subtotal"], errors="coerce")
        estimate = pd.to_numeric(part["predicted_full_24h_reward"], errors="coerce")
        comparable = _comparable_numeric_frame(truth, estimate)
        rows.append(
            {
                "participant_id": str(participant_id),
                "reward_rmse": _safe_rmse(truth, estimate),
                "daily_correlation": _safe_corr(truth, estimate),
                "reward_windows_compared": int(len(comparable)),
            }
        )
    return pd.DataFrame(rows)


def summarize_daily_heldout_metrics_by_participant(hourly_frame: pd.DataFrame, reward_frame: pd.DataFrame) -> pd.DataFrame:
    if (
        hourly_frame.empty
        or reward_frame.empty
        or "participant_id" not in hourly_frame.columns
        or "participant_id" not in reward_frame.columns
    ):
        return pd.DataFrame(columns=["participant_id", "daily_rmse_sqrtm", "daily_rmse", "heldout_daily_correlation", "daily_windows_used"])
    rows: list[dict[str, object]] = []
    for participant_id in sorted(set(reward_frame["participant_id"].astype(str))):
        hourly_part = hourly_frame.loc[hourly_frame["participant_id"].astype(str) == str(participant_id)].reset_index(drop=True)
        reward_part = reward_frame.loc[reward_frame["participant_id"].astype(str) == str(participant_id)].reset_index(drop=True)
        detail = build_heldout_subtotal_benchmark_frame(
            hourly_part,
            reward_part,
            truth_column="fitbit_truth_for_eval",
            estimate_column="latent_fitbit_scale_steps",
            mask_flag_column="fitbit_masked_for_eval",
        )
        if detail.empty:
            rows.append(
                {
                    "participant_id": str(participant_id),
                    "daily_rmse_sqrtm": np.nan,
                    "daily_rmse": np.nan,
                    "heldout_daily_correlation": np.nan,
                    "daily_windows_used": 0,
                }
            )
            continue
        truth = pd.to_numeric(detail["heldout_true_subtotal"], errors="coerce")
        estimate = pd.to_numeric(detail["heldout_predicted_subtotal"], errors="coerce")
        normalized_error = pd.to_numeric(detail["heldout_subtotal_error_sqrtm"], errors="coerce")
        rows.append(
            {
                "participant_id": str(participant_id),
                "daily_rmse_sqrtm": float(np.sqrt(np.nanmean(np.square(normalized_error)))) if normalized_error.notna().any() else np.nan,
                "daily_rmse": _safe_rmse(truth, estimate),
                "heldout_daily_correlation": _safe_corr(truth, estimate),
                "daily_windows_used": int(_comparable_numeric_frame(truth, estimate).shape[0]),
            }
        )
    return pd.DataFrame(rows)


def build_model_level_metric_tables(
    masked_fits: dict[tuple[str, str, str], dict[str, object]],
    parameter_summary: pd.DataFrame,
) -> tuple[pd.DataFrame, pd.DataFrame, pd.DataFrame]:
    metric_rows: list[pd.DataFrame] = []
    for (model_family, pooling_mode, mask_id), artifact in masked_fits.items():
        hourly_metrics = summarize_hourly_metrics_by_participant(artifact.get("hourly_prediction_frame", pd.DataFrame()))
        reward_metrics = summarize_reward_metrics_by_participant(artifact.get("reward_window_frame", pd.DataFrame()))
        daily_metrics = summarize_daily_heldout_metrics_by_participant(
            artifact.get("hourly_prediction_frame", pd.DataFrame()),
            artifact.get("reward_window_frame", pd.DataFrame()),
        )
        combined = hourly_metrics.merge(reward_metrics, how="outer", on="participant_id").merge(
            daily_metrics,
            how="outer",
            on="participant_id",
        )
        if combined.empty:
            continue
        combined["model_family"] = str(model_family)
        combined["pooling_mode"] = str(pooling_mode)
        combined["mask_id"] = str(mask_id)
        metric_rows.append(combined)

    participant_metric_frame = pd.concat(metric_rows, ignore_index=True) if metric_rows else pd.DataFrame()
    if participant_metric_frame.empty:
        empty = pd.DataFrame()
        return empty, empty, empty

    model_metric_summary = (
        participant_metric_frame.groupby(["model_family", "pooling_mode"], as_index=False)
        .agg(
            hourly_rmse=("hourly_rmse", "mean"),
            reward_rmse=("reward_rmse", "mean"),
            daily_rmse_sqrtm=("daily_rmse_sqrtm", "mean"),
            mean_panel_unit_loglik=("participant_id", lambda _s: np.nan),
            n_participants=("participant_id", "nunique"),
        )
    )

    correlation_summary = (
        participant_metric_frame.groupby(["model_family", "pooling_mode"], as_index=False)
        .agg(
            hourly_correlation_mean=("hourly_correlation", "mean"),
            hourly_correlation_min=("hourly_correlation", "min"),
            hourly_correlation_max=("hourly_correlation", "max"),
            daily_correlation_mean=("daily_correlation", "mean"),
            daily_correlation_min=("daily_correlation", "min"),
            daily_correlation_max=("daily_correlation", "max"),
            n_participants=("participant_id", "nunique"),
        )
    )

    parameter_stats = pd.DataFrame()
    if not parameter_summary.empty and {"model_family", "pooling_mode"}.issubset(parameter_summary.columns):
        parameter_stats = (
            parameter_summary.groupby(["model_family", "pooling_mode"], as_index=False)
            .agg(
                mean_panel_unit_loglik=("panel_unit_loglik", "mean"),
                mean_phi=("phi", "mean"),
                mean_sigma=("sigma", "mean"),
                mean_k=("k_fitbit", "mean"),
                sd_k=("k_fitbit", "std"),
            )
        )

    model_metric_summary = model_metric_summary.drop(columns=["mean_panel_unit_loglik"], errors="ignore").merge(
        parameter_stats,
        how="left",
        on=["model_family", "pooling_mode"],
    )
    return model_metric_summary, correlation_summary, participant_metric_frame


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class CovariateSpec:
    key: str
    label: str
    source_col: str
    beta_param: str
    description: str
    standardize: bool = True


COVARIATE_SPECS = {
    "trail_steps_24h": CovariateSpec(
        key="trail_steps_24h",
        label="Trailing 24h steps",
        source_col="cov_trail_steps_24h",
        beta_param="beta_steps24",
        description="Sum of Fitbit steps over the prior 24 hours.",
    ),
    "trail_sleep_minutes_24h": CovariateSpec(
        key="trail_sleep_minutes_24h",
        label="Trailing 24h sleep minutes",
        source_col="cov_trail_sleep_minutes_24h",
        beta_param="beta_sleep24",
        description="Sum of Fitbit sleep minutes over the prior 24 hours.",
    ),
    "trail_hr_mean_24h": CovariateSpec(
        key="trail_hr_mean_24h",
        label="Trailing 24h heart rate mean",
        source_col="cov_trail_hr_mean_24h",
        beta_param="beta_hr24",
        description="Average Fitbit heart rate over the prior 24 hours.",
    ),
    "trail_mood_mean_24h": CovariateSpec(
        key="trail_mood_mean_24h",
        label="Trailing 24h mood mean",
        source_col="cov_trail_mood_mean_24h",
        beta_param="beta_mood24",
        description="Average mood score over the prior 24 hours.",
    ),
    "send_lag_0_3h": CovariateSpec(
        key="send_lag_0_3h",
        label="Message sent 0 to 3 hours ago",
        source_col="cov_send_lag_0_3h",
        beta_param="eta_send_0_3h",
        description="Indicator that the most recent message was in the current hour or previous 3 hours.",
        standardize=False,
    ),
    "send_lag_4_11h": CovariateSpec(
        key="send_lag_4_11h",
        label="Message sent 4 to 11 hours ago",
        source_col="cov_send_lag_4_11h",
        beta_param="eta_send_4_11h",
        description="Indicator that the most recent message was 4 to 11 hours ago.",
        standardize=False,
    ),
    "send_lag_12_23h": CovariateSpec(
        key="send_lag_12_23h",
        label="Message sent 12 to 23 hours ago",
        source_col="cov_send_lag_12_23h",
        beta_param="eta_send_12_23h",
        description="Indicator that the most recent message was 12 to 23 hours ago.",
        standardize=False,
    ),
}


@dataclass
class HourlyStepModelData:
    participant_id: str
    study_user_id: str | None
    hourly_frame: pd.DataFrame
    covariate_keys: list[str]
    covariate_params: dict[str, str]
    covariate_standardization: dict[str, dict[str, float]]
    decision_window_frame: pd.DataFrame
    dropped_covariates: list[str]
    start_utc: pd.Timestamp
    end_utc: pd.Timestamp
    assumed_timezone: str

    @property
    def model_covariate_columns(self) -> list[str]:
        return [COVARIATE_SPECS[key].source_col for key in self.covariate_keys]

    @property
    def ys(self) -> pd.DataFrame:
        return self.hourly_frame.set_index("time_index")[["fitbit_steps_obs"]]

    @property
    def covars(self) -> pd.DataFrame:
        columns = ["baseline_log_mean"] + self.model_covariate_columns
        return self.hourly_frame.set_index("time_index")[columns]


@dataclass
class StepPompFitResult:
    initial_params: dict[str, float]
    fitted_params: dict[str, float]
    loglik: float
    pomp_object: Pomp | None
    mif_summary: pd.DataFrame
    mif_traces: pd.DataFrame
    pfilter_summary: pd.DataFrame
    timing_frame: pd.DataFrame
    runtime_breakdown: pd.DataFrame


@dataclass
class StepPompRunResult:
    initial_params: dict[str, float]
    fitted_params: dict[str, float]
    loglik: float
    pomp_object: Pomp | None
    mif_summary: pd.DataFrame
    mif_traces: pd.DataFrame
    pfilter_summary: pd.DataFrame
    filtered_states: pd.DataFrame
    plot_frame: pd.DataFrame
    reward_frame: pd.DataFrame
    timing_frame: pd.DataFrame
    runtime_breakdown: pd.DataFrame


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class GlobalSearchStageConfig:
    name: str
    n_starts: int
    keep_top: int
    particles: int
    mif_iterations: int
    cooling_fraction: float
    rw_sd_scale: float
    evaluation_particles: int
    evaluation_pfilter_reps: int
    n_fresh_random_starts: int = 0
    n_jittered_restarts: int = 0


@dataclass
class GlobalSearchResult:
    best_fit: StepPompFitResult
    best_run: StepPompRunResult
    candidate_frame: pd.DataFrame
    stage_summary: pd.DataFrame
    start_box_frame: pd.DataFrame
    total_runtime_seconds: float


def clone_hourly_step_model_data(data: HourlyStepModelData) -> HourlyStepModelData:
    return copy.deepcopy(data)


def _copy_frame(frame: pd.DataFrame) -> pd.DataFrame:
    return frame.copy(deep=True)


def strip_step_pomp_fit_result(result: StepPompFitResult) -> StepPompFitResult:
    return StepPompFitResult(
        initial_params=copy.deepcopy(result.initial_params),
        fitted_params=copy.deepcopy(result.fitted_params),
        loglik=float(result.loglik),
        pomp_object=None,
        mif_summary=_copy_frame(result.mif_summary),
        mif_traces=_copy_frame(result.mif_traces),
        pfilter_summary=_copy_frame(result.pfilter_summary),
        timing_frame=_copy_frame(result.timing_frame),
        runtime_breakdown=_copy_frame(result.runtime_breakdown),
    )


def strip_step_pomp_run_result(result: StepPompRunResult) -> StepPompRunResult:
    return StepPompRunResult(
        initial_params=copy.deepcopy(result.initial_params),
        fitted_params=copy.deepcopy(result.fitted_params),
        loglik=float(result.loglik),
        pomp_object=None,
        mif_summary=_copy_frame(result.mif_summary),
        mif_traces=_copy_frame(result.mif_traces),
        pfilter_summary=_copy_frame(result.pfilter_summary),
        filtered_states=_copy_frame(result.filtered_states),
        plot_frame=_copy_frame(result.plot_frame),
        reward_frame=_copy_frame(result.reward_frame),
        timing_frame=_copy_frame(result.timing_frame),
        runtime_breakdown=_copy_frame(result.runtime_breakdown),
    )


def strip_global_search_result(result: GlobalSearchResult) -> GlobalSearchResult:
    return GlobalSearchResult(
        best_fit=strip_step_pomp_fit_result(result.best_fit),
        best_run=strip_step_pomp_run_result(result.best_run),
        candidate_frame=_copy_frame(result.candidate_frame),
        stage_summary=_copy_frame(result.stage_summary),
        start_box_frame=_copy_frame(result.start_box_frame),
        total_runtime_seconds=float(result.total_runtime_seconds),
    )


analysis_start = normalize_timestamp_to_utc(analysis_start_utc_iso)
analysis_end = normalize_timestamp_to_utc(analysis_end_utc_iso)
covariate_keys = list(participant_covariate_keys)
free_params = list(estimated_free_params)

initial_param_overrides = {
    "phi": float(initial_phi),
    "sigma": float(initial_sigma),
    "k_fitbit": float(initial_k_fitbit),
}

local_if2_controls = {
    "particles": int(local_mle_particles),
    "mif_iterations": int(local_mle_mif_iterations),
    "random_seed": int(local_mle_random_seed),
    "cooling_fraction": float(local_mle_cooling_fraction),
    "rw_sd_scale": float(local_mle_rw_sd_scale),
    "evaluation_particles": int(local_mle_evaluation_particles),
    "evaluation_pfilter_reps": int(local_mle_evaluation_pfilter_reps),
}

global_search_start_box = {
    "phi": (float(global_start_box_phi_low), float(global_start_box_phi_high)),
    "sigma": (float(global_start_box_sigma_low), float(global_start_box_sigma_high)),
    "k_fitbit": (float(global_start_box_k_fitbit_low), float(global_start_box_k_fitbit_high)),
}

global_search_controls = {
    "random_seed": int(global_search_random_seed),
    "include_current_theta_as_start": bool(global_search_include_current_theta_as_start),
    "jitter_scale": float(global_search_jitter_scale),
    "box_shrink_factor_per_stage": float(global_search_box_shrink_factor_per_stage),
}

global_search_stage_controls = [
    {
        "name": str(global_stage1_name),
        "n_starts": int(global_stage1_n_starts),
        "keep_top": int(global_stage1_keep_top),
        "particles": int(global_stage1_particles),
        "mif_iterations": int(global_stage1_mif_iterations),
        "cooling_fraction": float(global_stage1_cooling_fraction),
        "rw_sd_scale": float(global_stage1_rw_sd_scale),
        "evaluation_particles": int(global_stage1_evaluation_particles),
        "evaluation_pfilter_reps": int(global_stage1_evaluation_pfilter_reps),
        "n_fresh_random_starts": int(global_stage1_n_fresh_random_starts),
        "n_jittered_restarts": int(global_stage1_n_jittered_restarts),
    },
    {
        "name": str(global_stage2_name),
        "n_starts": int(global_stage2_n_starts),
        "keep_top": int(global_stage2_keep_top),
        "particles": int(global_stage2_particles),
        "mif_iterations": int(global_stage2_mif_iterations),
        "cooling_fraction": float(global_stage2_cooling_fraction),
        "rw_sd_scale": float(global_stage2_rw_sd_scale),
        "evaluation_particles": int(global_stage2_evaluation_particles),
        "evaluation_pfilter_reps": int(global_stage2_evaluation_pfilter_reps),
        "n_fresh_random_starts": int(global_stage2_n_fresh_random_starts),
        "n_jittered_restarts": int(global_stage2_n_jittered_restarts),
    },
]

local_controls_display = (
    pd.DataFrame(
        [
            {
                "Particles": local_if2_controls["particles"],
                "IF2 iterations": local_if2_controls["mif_iterations"],
                "Cooling fraction": local_if2_controls["cooling_fraction"],
                "RW SD scale": local_if2_controls["rw_sd_scale"],
                "Evaluation particles": local_if2_controls["evaluation_particles"],
                "Evaluation pfilter reps": local_if2_controls["evaluation_pfilter_reps"],
            }
        ],
        index=["Local"],
    )
    .transpose()
    .reset_index()
)
local_controls_display.columns = ["Setting", "Local"]

global_stage_controls_display = (
    pd.DataFrame(
        [
            {
                "Stage": stage["name"],
                "Starts": stage["n_starts"],
                "Keep top": stage["keep_top"],
                "Particles": stage["particles"],
                "IF2 iterations": stage["mif_iterations"],
                "Cooling fraction": stage["cooling_fraction"],
                "RW SD scale": stage["rw_sd_scale"],
                "Evaluation particles": stage["evaluation_particles"],
                "Evaluation pfilter reps": stage["evaluation_pfilter_reps"],
                "Fresh random starts": stage["n_fresh_random_starts"],
                "Jittered restarts": stage["n_jittered_restarts"],
            }
            for stage in global_search_stage_controls
        ]
    )
    .set_index("Stage")
    .transpose()
    .reset_index()
    .rename(columns={"index": "Setting"})
)
global_start_box_display = pd.DataFrame(
    [
        {
            "Parameter": name,
            "Low": bounds[0],
            "High": bounds[1],
        }
        for name, bounds in global_search_start_box.items()
    ]
)

print(
    render_side_by_side_tables_latex(
        [
            {
                "caption": "Local IF2 controls",
                "width": 0.27,
                "df": local_controls_display,
                "precision": 3,
            },
            {
                "caption": "Global IF2 stage controls",
```
